rulings.Case Title: JENNIFER HASEGAWA ET AL VS. JIM ROWLAND ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order To: 1) Compel Plaintiffs Responses To: A) Form Interrogatories (Set One); B) Special Interrogatories (Set One); And C) Request For Production Of Documents (Set One); Without Objections And 2) Impose Monetary Sanctions On Plaintiffs And Their Attorney William Simpich For Misuse Of Discovery
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, March 29, 2017 line 1. DEFENDANTs JIM ROWLAND and MARGARET ROWLAND's Motion For An Order To: 1) Compel Plaintiffs Responses To: A) Form Interrogatories (Set One); B) Special Interrogatories (Set One); And C) Request For Production Of Documents (Set One); Without Objections And 2) Impose Monetary Sanctions On Plaintiffs And Their Attorney William Simpich For Misuse Of Discovery GRANTED. No opposition filed. Complete verified responses without objections must be provided within 15 days of notice of entry of order. Sanctions granted in the amount of $1,060. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: WAN FEN TAN VS. RICARDO IAN SUMMERS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Appointment Of Partition Referee
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, March 29, 2017 line 2. PLAINTIFF WAN TAN's Motion For Appointment Of Partition Referee GRANTED. No opposition filed. Brian McCaffrey is appointed referee. Moving party prepare order.= (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: VINCENT T.C. TAI VS. JOHN M. SANGER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Waterview Development Llc To Intervene
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, March 29, 2017 line 3. INTERVENOR WATERVIEW DEVELOPMENT, LLC's Motion Of Waterview Development Llc To Intervene DENIED in part and CONTINUED in part. Motion for mandatory intervention is DENIED. Moving party provides no authority or evidence that its interests are not adequately represented by the existing parties. Motion for discretionary leave to intervene is continued to July 7, 2017. Plaintiff and the moving party to file briefs (not to exceed 3 pages) to address the outcome of trial in action 536038 no later than July 3, 2017 by 4:00 p.m.= (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID MANN VS. MONTY BRACHA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, March 29, 2017 line 4. DEFENDANT MONTY BRACHA's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT Continued to April 27, 2017. Opposing party is ordered to comply with SFLR 2.7B and provide courtesy copy of opposition to department 501 no later than march 30, 2017 with a cover letter reflecting new hearing date.= (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: PATRICK M. COLLINS ET AL VS. CHRISTINA LI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION / Notice Of Motion & Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, March 29, 2017 line 9. DEFENDANTs CHRISTINA LI and HUOYING LIAN Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Request For Sanctions GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion is denied as to requests re: loan documents and Deeds of Trust. Motion is otherwise granted. Requests for sanctions are denied. Moving party prepare order listing the specific interrogatories to which motions is granted or denied in light of the ruling.= (501/REQ)
